candidates.item: Huupe launched as one of the first smart basketball systems to combine hardware and software into a holistic experience.
candidates.why_interesting: Goji Labs built the app and supporting systems for Huupe — billed as the world's first smart basketball hoop, featuring a digital screen on the backboard, a lidar camera for shot tracking, and the ability to stream content and compete globally in real time. It's a wildly specific, highly tangible product (a basketball hoop as a connected game console) that illustrates Goji Labs can handle complex hardware-software integration, not just typical mobile apps.
candidates.recency_note: Case study is current; Huupe product is live and sold at Best Buy.

candidates.item: Prediction Strike - The worlds first stock market for professional athletes.
candidates.why_interesting: Goji Labs designed and built PredictionStrike — a platform where fans buy and sell virtual shares of professional athletes whose price moves with real-world on-field performance. The concept (treating athletes like publicly traded stocks) is a genuinely novel idea, and PredictionStrike later raised $10M in a Series A. It's a concrete example of Goji Labs building category-defining, legally novel fintech-meets-sports products.
candidates.recency_note: PredictionStrike raised $10M Series A (reported September 2023); platform is live.

candidates.item: Goji Labs partnered with k-ID to develop the first cross-platform, tokenized child safety verification platform... The system was engineered to handle up to 50,000 verifications per minute, targeting scalability for up to 100 million monthly users. Within a year of launch, k-ID secured $51 million in funding and established partnerships with major companies like Google, Roblox, and Netflix.
candidates.why_interesting: An 11–50 person LA agency quietly built the technical backbone of a child-safety compliance platform that went on to partner with Google, Roblox, and Netflix and raised $51M. The infrastructure spec (50k verifications/minute, 100M monthly users target) is unusually demanding for a boutique agency engagement — it signals serious enterprise-grade engineering, not typical startup MVP work.
candidates.recency_note: k-ID is actively operating; partnerships with Google/Roblox/Netflix are confirmed from third-party sources.

candidates.item: Notable projects include developing an AI-powered dumbbell that sold out its presale in 45 minutes.
candidates.why_interesting: The product in question is Kabata — AI-powered dumbbells (world's first) with embedded sensors that auto-adjust weight and coach in real time. The presale sold out in 45 minutes. For a hardware-adjacent IoT + mobile project, this is a striking launch result that shows Goji Labs isn't just doing SaaS dashboards — they ship products people rush to buy.
candidates.as_of_date_if_known: Unknown exact date; Kabata product is live as of 2024–2025
candidates.recency_note: Kabata website confirms sold-out status multiple times; TechRadar covered in October 2024.

candidates.item: And, in the end, we saw fantastic tangible results: a notable increase in new mobile users by 26%, a rise in site-generated purchases by 30%, and an improved average time spent on the page by 18%.
candidates.why_interesting: Goji Labs rebuilt the WWF donation platform — consolidating four separate sites into one — and produced measurable nonprofit impact: +30% site-generated purchases (donations), +26% new mobile users. This is concrete, verified outcome data from one of the world's most recognized nonprofits, not a vague 'we improved UX' claim. It's useful proof that strategy-led design work moves real revenue/donation numbers.
candidates.as_of_date_if_known: Case study page last updated May 2025
candidates.recency_note: Published case study; WWF is a long-standing client reference.

candidates.item: The company has launched over 500 products used by more than 25 million people worldwide, supporting clients that have collectively raised over $1 billion in funding.
candidates.why_interesting: The '25 million people' end-user number is a more grounded and striking figure than the $1B funding stat — it makes the portfolio's real-world scale tangible. Combined with Clutch naming them a 2026 Top 100 Fastest-Growing company (April 2026, most recent recognition), it corroborates that Goji Labs is actively growing, not coasting on past reputation.
candidates.recency_note: Very recent — published April 2026, six weeks ago from today.

candidates.item: Too many non-technical founders were skipping strategy and jumping straight into development. This was the clear gap that Goji Labs sought to resolve when it was founded in 2014 as a two-person team of software engineers.
candidates.why_interesting: The founding story — two engineers who noticed non-technical founders wasting money by skipping strategy and going straight to code — is a blunt, self-aware origin narrative. It explains why they lead every engagement with strategy before writing a line of code. That's a specific, opinionated stance, not just an agency positioning line, and it's told in plain first-person language rather than polished marketing copy.
candidates.recency_note: Published May 2025; tied to 10-year milestone event.
